'''Christopher Andrew Hill''' (born 3 November 1987) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer.

He previously played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]],<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]], and represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level.

==Early career==
Hill signed for Leigh from local [[Wigan]] amateur club New Springs Lions in 2004, and started his career as a {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|lf}} before moving to {{rlp|pr}}, progressing through Leigh's service area, scholarship and youth ranks before moving into the first team at the [[Leigh Centurions]].

==Playing career==
===Leigh Centurions===
He made his first senior appearance for Leigh in the 2005 [[Super League X]] season, during a 8–74 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].

In 2006, Hill won the [[Northern Rail Cup]] with Leigh after consolidating his place in the side. He toured Australia with [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] Under-18s in this same year.

2011 saw Hill win his second [[Northern Rail Cup]] trophy with [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], featuring as [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] were defeated 16-20 at [[Blackpool]]'s [[Bloomfield Road]] Stadium.<ref name="Northern Rail Match Review">{{cite web|url=http://www.northernrail.org/northernrailcup/2011/thefinal|title=Northern Rail Match Review|publisher=www.northernrail.org|date=2011-04-18|accessdate=2012-08-26}}</ref>

Hill accumulated a couple of accolades with Leigh; firstly in reaching 100 appearances for the club, and also being the most recent {{rlp|pr}}-forward for Leigh to score a hat-trick of tries. After missing only one game in two years, he was made captain of Leigh in 2010.<ref name="rleague.com profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/h/hill_chris/index.php|title=rleague.com profile|publisher=rleague.com|date=2010-04-06|accessdate=2010-04-06|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120617175623/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/s/scott_beau/index.php|archivedate=2012-06-17}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
In 2011, Hill's impressive displays in the Championship saw him earn a three-year deal with [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in Super League, which saw him join the Warrington squad in 2012, remaining with [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] on a dual registration until the end of the 2011 season. In light of this, his then-coach [[Ian Millward]] predicted future national caps for Hill, with his nurturing at Warrington.
{{cquote|''I am delighted for Chris. As the fans will testify he has developed all season and is ready for Super League. It would not surprise me if in due course he won national recognition.''<ref name="Hill agrees Warrington switch">{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/article/hill-agrees-warrington-switch/22851|title=Hill agrees Warrington switch|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2011-07-08|accessdate=2011-07-11}}</ref>}}

Hill was selected to start in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|accessdate=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|accessdate=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

At the end of Hill's first season with Warrington, Hill was named as player of the season, players’ player of the season and won the Vice President's award, thus taking each of the major accolades at the Warrington end of season awards night.<ref name="warringtonguardian.co.uk - Chris is king of the Hills">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/9913456.print|title=Chris is king of the Hills|publisher=warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=2012-09-06|accessdate=2012-09-25}}</ref>

Hill played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/>
Hill played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |accessdate=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archivedate=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=13 November 2019|archive-date=27 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140527104413/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref>

In 2016, Hill was named club captain of the Warrington outfit. This was a successful year overall for the Warrington club under Hill, as they reached both finals and won their first piece of silverware since 2016, when they won the [[League Leaders' Shield]], the second time in the club's history. Hill was named in the Dream team for the season, and was also the Top Metre Maker of the season. He was rewarded for this amazing season with a contract extension with the club, which will keep him with the Warrington club until at least November 2021.  Hill played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>

Hill played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>

Hill played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>
Hill played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>
[[File:Chris Hill Warrington 2019.jpg|thumb|right|Hill leading the Warrington Wolves out at [[Anfield]] in 2019]]
Hill played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 10 September 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=10 Sep 2021|accessdate=27 Sep 2021|title=Chris Hill: Huddersfield sign Warrington's England prop Chris Hill on a two-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58512355}}</ref> On 14 April 2022, he made his 500th career appearance in a 20–20 draw against [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Chris Hill's reaction to his 500th appearance and promises "a few more" |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/chris-hills-reaction-to-his-500th-appearance-and-promises-a-few-more-51909/ |publisher=Serious About Rugby League |access-date=5 November 2022 |date=16 April 2022}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Hill played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 25 of the 2022 Super League season, Hill was controversially sin binned in Huddersfield's 18-14 loss to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leeds-live.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/phil-clarke-richie-myler-leeds-24847815/|title='It's a disgrace' - Phil Clarke's fury after controversial Richie Myler incident|website=www.leeds-live.co.uk}}</ref>
Hill played 24 games for Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>
Hill featured in 19 games for Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season as the club finished 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 2 October 2024, it was reported that Hill had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the Super League.<ref>{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Live|date=2 Oct 2024|accessdate=23 Oct 2024|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Salford Red Devils snap up Chris Hill as England veteran goes round again|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-red-devils-chris-hill-30057906}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 1 August 2025 it was reported that Hill had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] following from the financial difficulties that [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] were facing. Hill was granted immediate release from his Salford contract to join the Bulls for the remainder of the 2025 season in order to try help push their title hopes in the [[Betfred Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Telegraph & Argus|date=4 Aug 2025|author=Nathan Atkinson|title=Bradford Bulls sign England prop Chris Hill from Salford|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/25364066.bradford-bulls-sign-england-prop-chris-hill-salford/}}</ref>

==Personal life==
In 2015, Hill set up a plumbing business, Premier Plumbing.<ref>{{cite web |title=Warrington Wolves star Chris Hill becomes a plumber |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/13633698.warrington-wolves-star-chris-hill-becomes-a-plumber/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=10 September 2022 |date=1 September 2015}}</ref> On 12 September 2019 Hill opened a bathroom showroom called Immerse Bathroom Showroom.<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/17894959.warrington-wolves-chris-hill-opens-immerse-bathroom-showroom/ | title=Wire star Chris Hill opens his own bathroom showroom near to the town centre }}</ref>

==International career==
Hill made his International début for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the [[2012 Autumn International Series]] match against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] in Wrexham.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wolves trio help England hammer Wales in Wrexham |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/10011831.wolves-trio-help-england-hammer-wales-in-wrexham/?action=complain&cid=10907381 |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=10 September 2022 |date=27 October 2012}}</ref>

In October and November 2013, Hill played in all five England matches of the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|Rugby League World Cup]]. At the end of the 2014 domestic season, Hill travelled to Australia as part of the England national rugby league team's [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] squad.

In the 2015 International period, Hill was selected in Steve McNamara's 24-man squad to take on [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] up in England in a [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|test-series]].<ref>{{cite news 
| last = 
| first = 
| authorlink = 
| title = MCNAMARA NAMES 24-MAN SQUAD FOR 2015 INTERNATIONAL SERIES
| work = 
| place =
| pages = 
| publisher = englandrl.co.uk
| date = 2015-10-11
| url = http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article/47305/mcnamara-names-24-man-squad-for
| format = 
| accessdate = 2015-10-24}}</ref> Before the series began England played a [[Test match (rugby league)|test match]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref name="England vs France 84-4 in record win">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-report/43791/england-v-france|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151026223631/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-report/43791/england-v-france|url-status=dead|archive-date=26 October 2015|title=England vs France |publisher=sportinglife.com|date=24 October 2015|accessdate=1 January 2016}}</ref> Hill was part of the team that defeated the 'Les Tricolores'.<ref name="England demolish France 84-4 in record win">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/43791/report|title=England demolish France 84-4 in record win|publisher=skysports.com|date=24 October 2015|accessdate=1 January 2016}}</ref>
The following year, Hill was selected in England's 24-man squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]].

In October 2017, he was selected in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England squad]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>

Hill was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test début in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|publisher=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>

Hill was named in the 2021 world cup squad for England. Hill played in every match at the tournament including England's 27-26 semi-final loss to Samoa.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2022/nov/12/england-samoa-rugby-league-world-cup-semi-final-live/|title=England 26-27 Samoa: Rugby League World Cup semi-final – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
In October and November 2023, Hill played in all three matches of Tonga's tour of England as the Three Lions won the series 3-0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2023/nov/06/england-tonga-series-world-cup-huddersfield-st-helens-leeds/|title=England's 3-0 win over Tonga shows why they are the third best team|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
